$comment: Configuration for the API surface parity tests (ApiSurfaceParityTests, ElementMethodInvocationTests). 'enabled' is flipped to true by the wa-upgrade process once expected-api-surface.json matches the version being implemented. Overrides document intentional naming deviations of the Blazor wrappers - typically idiomatic .NET PascalCase word splits (e.g. maxlength -> MaxLength, autofocus -> AutoFocus) that differ from mechanical kebab-case conversion; ignores document intentional omissions (each ignore should carry a reason in 'ignoreReasons'). 'nativeElementMethods' lists DOM methods valid on any element; per-component 'extraElementMethods' allowlists element methods that exist in the Web Awesome source but are not CEM-documented - these MUST be re-verified against the target version's source on every upgrade (the CEM diff cannot see them).

ignoreReasons.title: native HTML attribute, passed through via AdditionalAttributes
ignoreReasons.did-ssr: internal SSR hydration marker set by Web Awesome server-side rendering (added to every component's base element in WA 3.3.0); not a consumer-authorable attribute
ignoreReasons.dir: native HTML global attribute, passed through via AdditionalAttributes (added to every component's base element in WA 3.3.0)
ignoreReasons.lang: native HTML global attribute, passed through via AdditionalAttributes (added to every component's base element in WA 3.3.0)
ignoreReasons.change: native change event folded into Blazor two-way binding (ValueChanged / @bind-Value)
ignoreReasons.name: form-control name is handled by Blazor InputBase form integration (NameAttributeValue / EditContext), not a discrete parameter
ignoreReasons.checked: checkbox checked state is the two-way-bound Value on WaInputBase<bool>
ignoreReasons.custom-error: the manually-set custom validity message (WA 3.3.0 form-associated validation) is managed imperatively through IFormValidation.SetCustomValidityAsync / ResetValidityAsync (JS interop), not an authorable attribute
ignoreReasons.formStateRestoreCallback: form-associated custom element (FACE) lifecycle callback the browser invokes to restore or autofill element state (WA 3.3.0); not consumer-facing and not marshalable from Blazor
ignoreReasons.plugins: Chart.js plugin registration is a JS-only array (typed never[] in the WA source, supplied via the chart's JSON <script> config slot); not an authorable HTML attribute
ignoreReasons.visiblePixelsInViewport: wa-page internal scroll-gap layout helper (takes a live HTMLElement arg; its own doc comment cites a StackOverflow scroll-fill fix) - not a consumer-facing API and not marshalable from Blazor
ignoreReasons.maxlength: exposed as MaxLength on WaInputBase (idiomatic .NET casing); mapped via attributeOverrides
ignoreReasons.minlength: exposed as MinLength on WaInputBase (idiomatic .NET casing); mapped via attributeOverrides
ignoreReasons.beforeinput: wa-number-input's cancelable before-value-change event (added in WA 3.6.0); exposed as the natural-PascalCase EventCallback OnBeforeInput (mechanical conversion would yield 'OnBeforeinput'), mapped via eventOverrides
ignoreReasons.getSymbol: function-valued property (custom symbol renderer callback), not an authorable HTML attribute
ignoreReasons.getMarked: static class method (WaMarkdown.getMarked() in markdown.d.ts) returning the shared Marked parser instance - a non-marshalable JS object, not a per-element instance method
ignoreReasons.updateAll: static class method (WaMarkdown.updateAll() in markdown.d.ts) that re-renders all connected wa-markdown instances after the shared Marked config changes - class-level, not a per-element instance method addressable via ElementReference interop
ignoreReasons.currentSlide: read-only JS state property of wa-carousel, not an authorable HTML attribute
ignoreReasons.slides: read-only JS collection property of wa-carousel, not an authorable HTML attribute
ignoreReasons.submenuOpen: read-only JS state property of wa-dropdown-item, not an authorable HTML attribute
ignoreReasons.extraElementMethods:stopObserver/startOb: observer control methods, browser-verified on the live WA 3.0.0 elements (2026-07-19) after the disconnect/reconnect rename incident and source-re-verified against WA 3.1.0 (2026-07-22), WA 3.2.0 (2026-07-23), WA 3.2.1 (2026-07-23), WA 3.3.0 (2026-07-23), WA 3.3.1 (2026-07-23), WA 3.4.0 (2026-07-23), WA 3.5.0 (2026-07-23) and WA 3.6.0 (2026-07-24), still present as private methods in mutation-observer.d.ts/resize-observer.d.ts; the CEM reports empty methods for both observers, so renames here are invisible to the upgrade diff - re-verify each upgrade
ignoreReasons.extraElementMethods:update: wa-relative-time refresh method, browser-verified on the live WA 3.0.0 element (2026-07-19) and source-re-verified against WA 3.1.0 (2026-07-22), WA 3.2.0 (2026-07-23), WA 3.2.1 (2026-07-23), WA 3.3.0 (2026-07-23), WA 3.3.1 (2026-07-23), WA 3.4.0 (2026-07-23), WA 3.5.0 (2026-07-23) and WA 3.6.0 (2026-07-24, the Lit ReactiveElement lifecycle update() inherited via WebAwesomeElement, hence absent from the component .d.ts - only updateTimeout appears in relative-time.d.ts), not CEM-documented
